芯片制造商高通公司今天宣布了一项重大收购：它将以14亿美元的价格收购总部位于圣克拉拉的硅公司Nuvia。高通公司打算在未来的芯片设计中使用Nuvia的技术，以用于从电话到汽车的各种设备。
 Nuvia由三位苹果前半导体高管于2019年创立。这家初创公司一直在为服务器开发定制的CPU核心设计，其公司资料经常提及“重新想象”的使命。硅设计。但是，高通公司认为Nuvia技术的应用程序将超越服务器。
 高通公司的新闻稿说，Nuvia将在CPU性能和电源效率方面实现阶跃功能改进，以满足下一代5G计算的需求。高通公司计划在旗舰智能手机，下一代笔记本电脑和数字座舱以及高级驾驶员辅助系统，扩展现实和基础设施网络解决方案中使用Nuvia的技术。
    与最近发布的Apple Silicon系列芯片一样，Nuvia的芯片也基于ARM架构，但未获得ARM的完全许可。这将使高通在开发能够帮助它与苹果芯片更直接竞争的芯片的同时获得更高的利润。高通公司已经为三星和微软设计的机器提供了基于ARM的芯片。
 根据Crunchbase的说法，规模较小的公司员工人数不到100名，因此，此次收购可能主要涉及知识产权。但是，新闻稿确实指出Nuvia的创始人和他们的雇员。将加入高通。
 该公告包括支持此次收购的声明，以及可能来自微软，华硕，谷歌，通用汽车和LG等众多科技公司的声明。换句话说，此次收购是高通及其客户和合作伙伴共同采取的战略的一部分，以对抗人们日益增长的关于苹果芯片更快，更高效的认识。
 在2019年，苹果公司起诉了Nuvia的一位创始人Gerard Williams III，声称他在离开苹果公司之前曾试图为新公司招募苹果员工。但是，该诉讼并未指控知识产权被盗。
note_en:
Chipmaker Qualcomm  announced a major acquisition today: it will buy Santa Clara-based silicon company Nuvia for $1.4 billion. Qualcomm intends to use Nuvia's technology in future chip designs for a wide range of devices, from phones to cars.
 Nuvia was founded in 2019 by three former Apple semiconductor executives. The startup has been developing custom CPU core design for servers, and its company materials make frequent reference to a mission to "reimagine" silicon design. But Qualcomm sees applications for Nuvia's tech beyond servers.
 Qualcomm's press release says Nuvia will deliver "step-function improvements in CPU performance and power efficiency to meet the demands of next-generation 5G computing." Qualcomm plans to use Nuvia's tech in "flagship smartphones, next-generation laptops, and digital cockpits, as well as Advanced Driver Assistance Systems, extended reality and infrastructure networking solutions."
    Like the recently launched Apple Silicon line of chips, Nuvia's chips are based on the ARM architecture but not fully licensed by ARM. This will allow Qualcomm to achieve better margins while developing chips that could help it compete with Apple's chips more directly. Qualcomm already provides ARM-based chips for machines designed by Samsung and Microsoft.
 The smaller company has fewer than 100 employees, according to  Crunchbase, so the acquisition may be chiefly about intellectual property. However, the press release does note that Nuvia's founders "and their employees" will join Qualcomm.
 The announcement included statements in support of the acquisition and where it might go from a wide range of tech companies, including Microsoft, Asus, Google, General Motors, and LG, among others. In other words, this acquisition is part of a strategy shared by Qualcomm and its customers and partners to battle growing perceptions that Apple's chips are faster and more efficient.
 In 2019, Apple sued one of Nuvia's founders, Gerard Williams III, claiming that he attempted to poach Apple employees for the new venture before he left his position at Apple. However, the suit did not allege intellectual property theft.
title: 高通将以14亿美元收购苹果高管成立的芯片公司
title_en: Qualcomm will acquire chip company founded by Apple execs for $1.4 billion

如今，SUV可能风行一时，但一些汽车制造商仍然对四门轿车心存余地，或者更准确地说，是他们的产品组合。其中包括本田在北美的高端子公司Ac歌，该公司最近推出了第二代TLX。但是请不要以为这是徽章工程的案例。第二代TLX是Ac歌（Acura）独有的-您不会在日本或欧洲发现它带有本田徽标。
 ura歌（Acura）通常被称为奢侈品牌，与雷克萨斯（Lexus）和英菲尼迪（Infiniti）争夺客户，但该公司的根基实际上比毛绒运动得多。 ura歌希望与这些传统重新建立联系，以使其与这些竞争对手区分开来。为此，它表示，售价40,000美元的新款2021 TLX是有史以来性能最为突出的轿车。它始于整体硬壳50％的硬壳式底盘，而悬架所连接的零件的刚度更高。车底有支撑，前减震器有更坚固的安装座，以及非常硬的中央通道。但与此同时，即使在这些高刚度区域中，也广泛使用铝来减轻旧型号的重量。
 除了运动目的之外，Ac歌还选择了前叉的双叉骨布置，它的横向刚度比第一代TLX上的MacPherson撑杆高85％。这意味着轮胎接触片在转弯时不会收缩太多。
 目前，TLX仅有一个发动机，即2.0L涡轮增压四缸发动机，可提供272hp和280lb-ft的功率。这使发动机的动力比旧型号的3.5升V6动力稍差，但涡轮增压四缸不仅整体扭矩更大，而且还具有更大的扭矩。它的扭矩平稳范围也更大，从1,600-4,500rpm。同样，只有TLX可以在前轮驱动或全轮驱动（Acuraspeak中的SH-AWD，以及我们测试的版本）中使用，只有一个变速箱选择-10速自动变速箱。
  我们已经在其他Acuras中体验了此SH-AWD系统的版本，例如RDX跨界车和NSX混合动力超级跑车。在此应用中，如果条件需要，最多可将70％的发动机扭矩传递到后轴，并将100％的扭矩传递到左轮或右轮。
 除了您需要查找是否需要AWD的$ 2,000之外，您还可能会损失一点燃油效率-FWD组合为25mpg（9.4l / 100km），AWD为24mpg（9.8l / 100km） 。
 与以往一样，每个人是否喜欢汽车的样式都会有自己的看法。该TLX在其前身的基础上已经有了很大的发展，现在已经接近其竞争对手Lexus ES的规模。轴距增加了3.7英寸（94毫米），总长度增加了2.9英寸（74毫米）。但是最引人注目的变化是增加了仪表板到前轴的长度，该长度增加了7.8英寸（198毫米）。 
当从侧面看时，这给了汽车相当不同的比例。它的整体宽度也更宽（2.2英寸/ 56毫米），并且比最后一部TLX的履带更宽-前轮相距1.2英寸（30毫米），后轮距1.6英寸（40毫米）。尽管在可能的情况下使用了轻质材料，路边重量也稍高一些-FWD TLX的秤重为3,702磅（1,679千克），而SH-AWD型号的磅重为3,920磅（1,778千克）。
 即使是更大的汽车，TLX实际上也很容易在道路上放置，这要归功于引擎盖长度上的突出折痕。正如我之前指出的那样，在车身面板上放置复杂的折痕是汽车制造商相互屈挠的方式之一，并且通过这种方式，TLX Acura的屈曲强度非常大（与底盘本身不同）。
  我喜欢TLX的内部。前排座椅非常舒适，后排空间也不错。 ura歌（Acura）坚持使用传统的模拟转盘作为主要乐器的显示屏，但也有一个10.5英寸的全彩平视显示器作为选装件。信息娱乐系统在仪表板上具有10.2英寸的显示屏，您可以通过中央控制台上的触摸板进行控制。
  如果您以前从未使用过Acura触摸板，那么乍一看可能会有些奇怪，因为触摸板和屏幕之间存在1：1的关系。但是稍微熟悉一下（一两天就可以做到），它就变得非常自然。最大的优点是您可以建立肌肉记忆，这意味着无需中断视线就可以操作信息娱乐系统。 Acura的本地系统非常好，并且具有直观的UI，并且Apple CarPlay和Android Auto适用于喜欢使用手机的用户。
 自2021年以来，您可以而且应该期待中型豪华车中全套先进的驾驶员辅助系统，而TLX并不令人失望。这一代产品增加了一种新型前排乘客安全气囊（我们最近进行了探索），交通标志识别，交通拥堵辅助系统，驾驶员监视系统以及行人感应和自动紧急制动。我从未经历过自动包围曝光激活，但是TLX的前向碰撞警告非常敏感，甚至有些跳动。有些人可能会觉得这太烦人了，但是对于像FCW这样的安全系统，我会过分渴望睡着了。
 至于驾驶体验，尽管它的操控性比上一代TLX更为锐利，但在Advance trim中，我仍然发现该车在方向改变时会有些沉闷，输入和反应之间的滞后时间太短。增加方向盘的重量可以部分缓解这种情况，但是我怀疑敏锐的驾驶员会希望选择A-Spec版本。
note_en:
The SUV may be ascendant these days, but some carmakers still have room in their hearts, or more accurately their product portfolios, for the four-door sedan. Among them is Acura, the high-end arm of Honda in North America, which recently debuted its second-generation TLX. But don't assume this is a case of badge engineering; the second-gen TLX is unique to Acura—you won't find it wearing a Honda logo in Japan or Europe.
 Acura is mostly known as a luxury brand, competing for customers with Lexus and Infiniti, but the company's roots are actually much more sporty than plush. Acura wants to reconnect with that heritage as a way of differentiating itself from those rivals, and to that end, it says the new $40,000 2021 TLX is the most performance-focused sedan it has ever made.  It starts with a monocoque chassis that's 50 percent stiffer overall, with even higher rigidity at the parts where the suspension is attached. There are braces for the underbody, more rigid mounts for the front dampers, and a very stiff center tunnel. But at the same time, there's extensive use of aluminum, even in some of these high-stiffness areas, to cut weight over the old model.
 Further to its sporting intentions, Acura opted for a double-wishbone arrangement for the front suspension, which it says has 85 percent more lateral stiffness than the MacPherson struts on the first-generation TLX. That means the tires' contact patches don't shrink too much under cornering.
 For now, there's just a single engine available for the TLX, a 2.0L turbocharged four-cylinder that provides 272hp and 280lb-ft. That makes the engine slightly less powerful than the 3.5L V6 in the old model, but the turbocharged four is not only more torquey overall; it also has a much broader torque plateau, from 1,600-4,500rpm. Similarly, there's only a single transmission choice—a 10-speed automatic transmission—although the TLX is available either in front-wheel drive or all-wheel drive (SH-AWD in Acuraspeak, and the version we tested).
  We've experienced versions of this SH-AWD system in other Acuras, like the  RDX crossover and  NSX hybrid supercar. In this application, up to 70 percent of the engine's torque can go to the rear axle, and 100 percent of  that torque can be sent to either the left or right wheel, should the conditions demand that.
 In addition to the $2,000 you'll need to find if you want AWD, you can also expect to lose a little fuel efficiency—25mpg (9.4l/100km) combined for FWD, 24mpg (9.8l/100km) combined for AWD.
 As ever, everyone will have their own take on whether or not they like the way a car is styled. This TLX has grown quite a bit over its predecessor and is now closer to the size of its competitor, the Lexus ES. The wheelbase has expanded by 3.7 inches (94mm), and the overall length has grown by 2.9 inches (74mm). But the most noticeable change is the increased dash-to-front axle length, which has grown by 7.8 inches (198mm).
 That gives the car a rather different proportion when viewed side-on. It's also wider overall (by 2.2 inches/56mm) and has a wider track than the last TLX—the front wheels are 1.2 inches (30mm) further apart, with the rears at 1.6 inches (40mm). Curb weight is also a little higher, despite the use of lightweight materials where possible—the FWD TLX tips the scales at 3,702lbs (1,679kg), and the SH-AWD variant comes in at 3,920lbs (1,778kg).
 Even though it's a bigger car, the TLX is actually quite easy to place on the road thanks to the prominent creases that run the length of the hood.  As I've noted before, putting complex creases into body panels is one of the ways car makers flex on each other, and with this, TLX Acura is flexing pretty hard (unlike the chassis itself).
  I liked the TLX's interior. The front seats are very comfortable, with decent room in the back as well. Acura is sticking with conventional analogue dials for the main instrument display, but there's also a 10.5-inch full-color heads-up display as an option. The infotainment system has a 10.2-inch display on the dash, which you control via a touchpad on the center console.
  If you've never used Acura's touchpad before, it can be a little strange at first, as there's a 1:1 relationship between the touchpad and the screen. But with a little familiarity—a day or two should do it—it becomes extremely natural. A big advantage is that you can build up muscle memory, which means being able to operate the infotainment without taking your eyes off the road. Acura's native system is perfectly fine and has an intuitive UI, and there's Apple CarPlay and Android Auto for those who prefer to use their phones.
 Since it's 2021, you can and should expect a full complement of advanced driver-assistance systems in a midsize luxury car, and the TLX does not disappoint. The additions for this generation are  a new kind of front passenger airbag (that we explored recently), traffic-sign recognition, traffic-jam assist, a driver-monitoring system, and pedestrian sensing and automatic emergency braking. I never experienced AEB activating, but the TLX's forward collision warning is very sensitive and even a little jumpy. Some might find that overly annoying, but with safety systems like FCW, I'll take overeager over asleep.
 As for the driving experience, while it is a sharper-handling car than the last TLX, in Advance trim I still found the car wallowed a little during direction changes, with just a little too much lag between input and reaction. This was partially ameliorated by increasing the weight of the steering, but I suspect keen drivers will want to opt for the A-Spec version.
title: ura歌TLX Advance击中了豪华元素，却错过了运动者
title_en: The Acura TLX Advance hits the luxury notes, misses the sporting ones

Google研究人员详细介绍了一种复杂的黑客操作，利用Chrome和Windows中的漏洞在Android和Windows设备上安装恶意软件。
 其中一些漏洞是零日漏洞，这意味着它们针对的漏洞当时是Google，Microsoft和大多数外部研究人员所不知道的（两家公司均已修复了安全漏洞）。黑客通过水坑攻击进行了攻击，这些攻击会破坏目标用户经常光顾的网站，并在网站上加上可在访客设备上安装恶意软件的代码。被欺骗的网站利用了两个漏洞利用服务器，一个用于Windows用户，另一个用于Android用户。
  使用零时差和复杂的基础设施本身并不表示复杂程度，但确实显示出专业的黑客团队的技能高于平均水平。结合攻击代码的鲁棒性（有效地将多个漏洞利用链接在一起），该活动证明了它是由“高度复杂的参与者”执行的。
 这些漏洞利用链旨在提高效率和Google的零项目漏洞利用研究小组的一位研究人员写道。 “它们是精心设计的，复杂的代码，具有各种新颖的利用方法，成熟的日志记录，复杂且经过计算的利用后利用技术以及大量的反分析和目标检查。我们相信，专家团队已经设计和开发了这些漏洞利用链。”
    研究人员说，有效载荷的模块化，可互换的利用链以及操作的日志记录，针对性和成熟度也使战役脱颖而出。
   攻击者通过利用Chrome的零日漏洞和最近修复的Chrome漏洞来执行远程代码执行。所有零时差都针对Windows用户使用。针对Android设备的攻击链中没有一个利用零日漏洞，但“零号计划”研究人员表示，攻击者很可能会使用Android零日漏洞。
 下图提供了该活动的直观概览，该活动发生在去年第一季度： 
总之，零号项目共分六期，详细介绍了研究人员发现的漏洞利用和漏洞利用后的有效负载。 其他部分概述了Chrome无限漏洞，Chrome漏洞利用，Android漏洞利用，Android后漏洞利用有效载荷和Windows漏洞利用。  该系列的目的是帮助整个安全社区更有效地打击复杂的恶意软件操作。 “我们希望这篇博客文章系列能使其他人深入了解来自现实世界，成熟且可能资源丰富的演员的剥削，”零号项目研究人员写道。
note_en:
Google researchers have detailed a sophisticated hacking operation that exploited vulnerabilities in Chrome and Windows to install malware on Android and Windows devices.
 Some of the exploits were zero-days, meaning they targeted vulnerabilities that at the time were unknown to Google, Microsoft, and most outside researchers (both companies have since patched the security flaws). The hackers delivered the exploits through watering-hole attacks, which compromise sites frequented by the targets of interest and lace the sites with code that installs malware on visitors’ devices. The boobytrapped sites made use of two exploit servers, one for Windows users and the other for users of Android.
  The use of zero-days and complex infrastructure isn’t in itself a sign of sophistication, but it does show above-average skill by a professional team of hackers. Combined with the robustness of the attack code—which chained together multiple exploits in an efficient manner—the campaign demonstrates it was carried out by a “highly sophisticated actor.”
 “These exploit chains are designed for efficiency & flexibility through their modularity,” a researcher with Google’s Project Zero exploit research team  wrote. “They are well-engineered, complex code with a variety of novel exploitation methods, mature logging, sophisticated and calculated post-exploitation techniques, and high volumes of anti-analysis and targeting checks. We believe that teams of experts have designed and developed these exploit chains.”
    The modularity of the payloads, the interchangeable exploit chains, and the logging, targeting, and maturity of the operation also set the campaign apart, the researcher said.
   The attackers obtained remote code execution by exploiting the Chrome zero-day and several recently patched Chrome vulnerabilities. All of the zero-days were used against Windows users. None of the attack chains targeting Android devices exploited zero-days, but the Project Zero researchers said it’s likely the attackers had Android zero-days at their disposal.
 The diagram below provides a visual overview of the the campaign, which occurred in the first quarter of last year:
  In all, Project Zero published six installments detailing the exploits and post-exploit payloads the researchers found. Other parts outline a  Chrome infinity bug, the  Chrome exploits, the  Android exploits, the  post-Android exploitation payloads, and the  Windows exploits.
 The intention of the series is to assist the security community at large in more effectively combating complex malware operations. “We hope this blog post series provides others with an in-depth look at exploitation from a real-world, mature, and presumably well-resourced actor,” Project Zero researchers wrote.
title: 黑客利用4个零日感染Windows和Android设备
title_en: Hackers used 4 zero-days to infect Windows and Android devices

亚马逊在周二为看似已失效的社交网络平台Parler提起的诉讼提供了收据，其中详细介绍了AWS。反复努力使Parler应对发布到服务中的明确暴力威胁。
 在上周三美国国会大厦发生暴动之后，AWS在周日午夜将Parler从其网络托管平台上撤下。作为回应，帕勒（Parler）提起诉讼，指责亚马逊出于政治原因违反合同，并与Twitter串通以驱使竞争对手下线。
  但是该禁令与扼杀观点无关。或＆＃34;阴谋＆＃34;为了限制竞争对手，亚马逊在其回应文件（PDF）中表示。亚马逊说，相反，这起案件是关于帕勒表现出的不愿和无能。删除包含危险内容的活动，包括煽动和策划强奸，酷刑和暗杀指定公职人员和私人公民的帖子... AWS暂停了Parler的帐户，作为阻止进一步访问的手段此类内容，包括旨在破坏即将到来的总统过渡的暴力计划。＆＃34;
 如果存在任何违规行为，则是Parler证明的失败和无法识别和删除此类内容的能力，＆＃34;亚马逊添加了。 ＆＃34;强制AWS托管计划，鼓励和煽动暴力的内容是前所未有的。
  对于外部观察者而言，Parler的迅速崛起及其在上周末和周末的彻底解压都可能让人感到非常突然。派勒（Parler）于2018年成立，但在几个月前的11月大选前后才获得真正的广泛关注。
    报道最初于12月开始浮出水面，称右翼边缘分子正在使用Parler和其他平台计划于1月6日在华盛顿特区举行某种形式的抗议或集会。全世界看到了这些“集会”如何;上周结果。
 上周五，在国会大厦发生事件之后，Google禁止Parler在Android应用商店中使用，理由是该平台未能删除诸如煽动暴力的帖子之类的令人讨厌的内容。一天后，苹果公司也因未能解决这些威胁到人们安全的威胁而类似地将Parler从iOS暂停。到周末结束时，Parler也从AWS那里获得了启动，并完全脱机。 
亚马逊说，但派勒并没有突然中断，而是警告了数月。亚马逊的备案文件包括11月中旬发送给Parler的电子邮件副本（PDF，种族诽谤的内容警告），其中包含有关民主党人的种族主义言论的截图，其中包括前第一夫人米歇尔·奥巴马，以及其他人的一系列回应。用户来杀死所有人。
 亚马逊提供了100多个额外的代表性内容。该公司表示，提倡在接下来的七个星期内对Parler施加暴力。文件中的另一份文件（PDF，也有针对种族歧视和暴力威胁的内容警告）列出了亚马逊从12月中旬开始向Parler报告的数十个帖子示例。这些职位要求除其他外：杀害特定的跨性别者；积极希望进行种族战争和谋杀黑人和犹太人；并杀害了一些积极分子和政治人物，例如史黛西·艾布拉姆斯（Stacey Abrams），众议员亚历山大·奥卡西奥·科尔特斯（Alexander Ocasio-Cortez）（民主党）和前总统巴拉克·奥巴马（Barack Obama）。
    1月8日至9日，AWS的代表与Parler执行领导就平台的内容审核政策，流程和工具进行了交谈。亚马逊说。作为回应，据称Parler提供了依赖于＆amp; volunteer＆＃34;的步骤。据称，Parler的执行情况适度，而Parler的首席执行官John Matze告诉AWS，＆＃34; Parler积压了26,000份报告内容，这些报告违反了其社区标准，并继续提供服务。＆＃34;
  不幸的是，至少有一些Parler用户造成的暴力威胁远非假设。
 在服务完全脱机之前，几乎所有Parler的内容都已存档。 Gizmodo记者调查了这些存档的数据后，找到了数百名Parler用户，这些用户在1月6日事件期间从国会大厦内或附近向平台发布了视频。
 在Reddit和Twitter上进行的临时工作，以收集来自Parler的屏幕截图和视频，也显示了在1月6日暴动前后的几天中，平台上提出的令人不安的威胁和主张模式。
 正如《华盛顿邮报》周二报道的那样，联邦调查局也非常清楚在线上存在可信的暴力威胁。 1月5日，在暴乱者袭击国会大厦的前一天，联邦调查局在弗吉尼亚州诺福克市的办公室发布了一份备忘录，部分内容是：“一个在线话题讨论了对暴力的具体呼吁，其中包括说要做好战斗的准备。 。国会需要听到玻璃破裂，门被踢开，BLM和Pantifa奴隶士兵流血的消息。变得暴力。停止称此为游行，集会或抗议。去那里准备战争。我们要当总统或死。没有别的可以实现这个目标。＆＃39;＆＃34; 
不幸的是，暴力威胁都没有减轻。 下周 的就职典礼 ， 因为 暴力 可信的威胁 继续瞄准 不仅 全国 ＆＃ 39 ; 哥伦比亚特区 正在慢慢 变成 总统当选人 拜登 ＆＃ 39的 堡垒 未来 的资本 ， 也是国家 的首都 为好。
title: 归档：亚马逊警告Parler数月关于“ 100多种”暴力威胁
title_en: Filing: Amazon warned Parler for months about “more than 100” violent threats

note: 我们一直与Linus在一起，与Markiplier发出不连贯的喊叫声，与LGR在一起，并与Scott Manley汇报了情况，但是随着2020年底的到来，我们还有最后一个视频要分享。  这次，我们与Dimitris Giannakis坐下来，后者更出名的是Modern Vintage Gamer。 我们通过与该细分市场的前几位明星一样的方式来运作MVG，我们为他准备了来自他的YouTube频道的一些最受欢迎和/或最晦涩的评论，并向他提出了质疑 他记得他们的程度。 （剧透警报，他做得很好-实际上，该视频包含了MVG关于在控制台硬件上进行黑客攻击的许多智慧之宝。）  MVG的频道也庆祝了骇客的喜悦，同时也是技术花絮的宝库。 在寒冷的12月下午，您可能要做的事情比倒转并观看一些视频还要糟糕！
note_en:
We've hung out with  Linus, made incoherent shouting noises with  Markiplier, chilled with  LGR, and debriefed with  Scott Manley—but we've got one last video to share as we roll into the end of 2020.
 This time we're sitting down with Dimitris Giannakis, better known as  Modern Vintage Gamer. We're running MVG through the same gauntlet as we did the previous stars of the segment—we came prepared with some of his most popular and/or most obscure comments from his YouTube channel, and we're quizzing him on how well he remembers them. (Spoiler alert, he does great—in fact, the video contains a lot of gems of wisdom from MVG about hacking on console hardware.)
 MVG's channel is a celebration of the joy of hacking and a treasure trove of tech tidbits, too. There are way worse things you could be doing on a chilly December afternoon than kicking back and watching some videos!
title: Modern Vintage Gamer谈论开发细节和硬件黑客
title_en: Modern Vintage Gamer talks dev details and hardware hackery

note:
CD Projekt Red仍在努力遏制上个月针对PS4和Xbox One发行的Cyber​​punk 2077版本中的重大技术问题的广泛报道所造成的损害。为此，工作室联合创始人兼首席执行官Marcin Iwinski在推特上发布了一条视频消息，试图解释导致发行问题的内部情况。 ＆＃34;尽管在PC上获得了好评，但Cyber​​punk 2077的控制台版本并未达到我们希望达到的质量标准，＆＃34;伊温斯基在邮件中说。我和整个领导团队对此深表歉意，而这段视频是我公开拥有的。＆＃34;
  伊温斯基说，问题的核心是“游戏中的流媒体系统”。赛博朋克2077曾经用来“喂养”内容和游戏机制，无需频繁中断加载。该系统必须“不断改进”。 Iwinski表示，在开发过程中使用的是最新一代的游戏机，以跟上＆＃34; epic＆＃34; PC版本的外观（它的图形和其他资产按比例缩小以在更有限的旧控制台硬件上工作）。
 “一开始看起来并不困难，但是，我认为时间证明了我们低估了这个任务，”伊温斯基说。 ＆＃34;因为城市是如此拥挤，而老式控制台的磁盘带宽正是它的本钱，所以它不断挑战着我们。
  对于为什么未发现控制台版本的这些问题，以及在启动之前已解决或延迟的问题，Iwinski不太直接。 ＆＃34;每项变更和改进都需要进行测试，事实证明，我们的测试并未显示您在玩游戏时遇到的大部分问题，＆＃34;伊温斯基说。他补充说，由于团队在COVID相关的限制下在家工作导致的通信问题，这意味着一些问题由于视频通话或电子邮件而丢失了。 ＆＃34;我们每天都看到重大改进，从而导致发布[并且]真的以为我们将在零[控制台版本]交付的那一天交付，＆＃34;他说。
    Iwinski说，这些开发后期的改进和对游戏机的不断测试是12月初未与PC代码一起提供给游戏者的游戏版本的评论代码的原因。虽然Iwinski说控制台代码是在12月8日发出的（比原计划的要晚），但Ars Technica在12月10日游戏发布之前没有提供任何控制台版本来进行测试（比起最初的PC审查禁运要少得多）在12月7日）。
    Iwinski的声明也没有解决最初在PC版本的游戏中发现的重大（但不足游戏性的）错误。正如我们在PC版本的评论中所指出的那样，非敌对角色将在空中一动不动，卡在一个荒谬的T型姿势中，或者无缘无故地在狭窄的走廊中来回走动。人们在我眼前逐步淘汰存在。俱乐部拥挤的桌子上将有一种饮料悬停在一个看不见的顾客手中。＆＃34;
  除了已经发布的三个修补程序外，Iwinski重申了对重大更新的承诺，即在10天内删除该更新。并在接下来的几周内进行了另一项重大更新。之后，开发团队将专注于先前承诺的免费PlayStation 5和Xbox Series S / X-native版本的免费DLC和免费更新，现在预计将在2021年下半年发布。 
伊温斯基（Iwinski）的最新消息是在12月的一次电话会议上发表的，在那次电话会议中，他承认CDPR领导团队忽略了需要额外时间在基本的上一代游戏机上改进游戏的信号。 自该电话以来，该游戏已从PlayStation和Xbox在线商店中除名，而CD Projekt Red则面临着股东不满的诉讼。
title: CDPR首席执行官将“游戏内流媒体”归咎于Cyberpunk的控制台问题
title_en: CDPR CEO blames “in-game streaming” for Cyberpunk’s console problems

周三，在德克萨斯州南部，SpaceX发射了其最新的Starship原型机一次或两次。相反，工程师和技术人员在不到四个小时的时间内就对其SN9原型机及其Raptor火箭发动机进行了加油和试验射击三次。
 在进行了三个测试中的第一个之后，SpaceX的创始人兼首席工程师埃隆·马斯克（Elon Musk）在推特上说，今天在SpaceX的是关于练习Starship引擎启动的信息。引擎开火时，船被大头钉压住。＆＃34;第三次测试后，马斯克确认所有三个测试均已完成，没有重大问题。
  快速连续执行三个这样的测试是一个显着的成就，它指出了SpaceX的星际飞船发射程序的硬件，地面系统和程序已经成熟。马斯克说，目标是达到一个可以跳进去走走的地步。去火星。如果能够实现，那么这种能力将在未来数年内实现。但是该公司似乎正在朝着强大的发射系统迈进。
 假设星期三的发动机测试得到的数据令人满意，那么SN9的下一步可能会继续发展。该车已经在发射台上了，马斯克和他的工程师现在将进行“准备就绪”工作。提前审查预计将进行约12.5公里的另一次试飞，类似于SN8车辆在12月进行的飞行。目标是在SN8经过原本接近完美的飞行之后在着陆点火热结束后成功着陆。
    SpaceX已获得美国联邦航空管理局（Federal Aviation Administration）的批准，于周四，周五或周六从其位于南德克萨斯州博卡奇卡海滩附近的设施发射SN9火箭。他们有可能尽快在周四将SN9转弯以进行飞行尝试。但是，人们担心高层风，至少在周六之前，这种风似乎是敌对的。
 消息人士指出，SpaceX迫切希望继续使用SN9，因为它已在附近的高架设施中完成了下一艘Starship原型机SN10的组装。该公司可能会尽快在1月底前试飞这辆车，因为它在不同建造状态下还有其他几辆原型车。
 近期目标是证明星舰的轨道飞行能力。为此，公司需要首先制造和测试“超级重型”火箭，这将是“星舰”的第一阶段。这项引人注目的测试将是将一艘星际飞船送入轨道，以确定其是否可以使用面向大气一侧的隔热砖组合安全返回地球。它将完成一系列复杂的动作以降低速度并最终进行受控着陆。 
在本月初发表的一次采访中，SpaceX总裁兼首席运营官格温妮·肖特韦尔（Gwynne Shotwell）告诉Ars，她认为这次轨道飞行试验很有可能在2021年进行。
note_en:
On Wednesday, in South Texas, SpaceX fired up its newest Starship prototype not once or twice. Rather, engineers and technicians fueled and test fired their SN9 prototype and its Raptor rocket engines three times in less than four hours.
 After the first of three tests, SpaceX founder and chief engineer Elon Musk said  on Twitter, "Today at SpaceX is about practicing Starship engine starts. Ship is held down by massive pins while engines are fired." After the third test,  Musk confirmed that all three tests were completed without a major issue.
  Performing three tests like this in rapid succession is a notable achievement, and it points to maturing spaceship hardware, ground systems, and procedures for SpaceX's Starship launch program. Musk said the goal is to reach a point where one could "hop in and go" to Mars. Such a capability remains years into the future, if it is ever realized. But the company appears to be making progress toward a robust launch system.
 Assuming data from Wednesday's engine tests is favorable, the next step for SN9 is likely to go and fly. The vehicle is already on its launch stand, and Musk and his engineers will now conduct a "readiness" review in advance of what is expected to be another test flight to about 12.5km, similar to the flight performed by the  SN8 vehicle in December. The goal will be to land successfully, after SN8 met a fiery end at the landing site after an otherwise near-perfect flight.
    SpaceX  has received approval from the Federal Aviation Administration to launch its SN9 vehicle on Thursday, Friday, or Saturday from its facility near Boca Chica Beach, in South Texas. It is possible they will be able to turn SN9 around for a flight attempt as soon as Thursday. However there are concerns about upper-level winds, which appear to be hostile until at least Saturday.
 Sources suggested that SpaceX is eager to get on with flying SN9, as it has nearly completed assembly of the next Starship prototype, SN10, at its nearby high bay facilities. The company is pressing ahead to fly this vehicle perhaps as soon as the end of January, as it has several other prototypes in various states of construction.
 The near-term goal is to demonstrate an orbital flight capability for Starship. To do this, the company needs to first build and test the Super Heavy rocket, which will serve as Starship's first stage. This dramatic test will see a Starship launched into orbit to determine whether it can safely return to Earth using a combination of heat shield tiles on its atmosphere-facing side. It will complete a complex set of maneuvers to bleed off speed and ultimately make a controlled landing.
 In an interview published earlier this month, SpaceX President and Chief Operating Officer Gwynne Shotwell told Ars she thought it was more likely than not that this orbital flight test would occur in 2021.
title: SpaceX热火在四个小时内对其星际飞船进行了三次测试
title_en: SpaceX hot fire tests its Starship vehicle three times in four hours

11月，我们获悉，美国国家公路交通安全管理局（National Highway Traffic Safety Administration）已对特斯拉流行的Model S和X电池电动汽车的潜在缺陷进行了工程分析。周三，另一只鞋掉了，当时美国国家公路交通安全管理局（NHTSA）通知这家美国汽车制造商，必须召回158,000辆汽车来修复有缺陷的触摸屏。
 问题与车辆中的组件有关。信息娱乐系统，称为媒体控制单元。 MCU内埋有一个8GB eMMC NAND闪存芯片，该芯片只能写入有限次。一旦达到此次数的读/写周期（大约需要三年到四年的时间，具体取决于汽车的行驶量），触摸屏就会死亡。不幸的是，在汽车中，这是一个真正的问题，因为触摸屏是几乎所有控件的访问方式。
  不能在您的汽车中浏览Internet或播放Podcast显然是一种不便，特别是在高端汽车中。但是，NHTSA更担心这样一个事实，即如果触摸屏死机，则备用摄像机和窗户除雾等功能也会丢失，其他车载安全系统的声音警报也会丢失。该问题在特斯拉社区中是众所周知的，影响到2012年至2018年之间制造的任何Model S以及2016年至2018年之间制造的ModelXs。NHTSA收到业主的537份投诉后，询问特斯拉是否了解有关该范围的更多信息。问题。它做到了—移交了数千个投诉的记录和超过12,000个MCU替换产品。
    据路透社报道，现在，NHTSA采取了不同寻常的步骤，告诉特斯拉必须召回158,000辆受影响的车辆。 ＆＃34; [D]在我们审查数据期间，特斯拉提供了确认，鉴于存储设备的有限存储容量，所有单元都将不可避免地发生故障，＆＃34; NHTSA告诉路透社。 NHTSA还指出，特斯拉曾尝试通过软件补丁来解决此问题，但这些补丁程序在程序上和实质上是不足的。法律要求汽车制造商召回存在安全缺陷的车辆。
 特斯拉没有新闻办公室可以发表评论，该公司也没有在其Twitter提要或首席执行官埃隆·马斯克（Elon Musk）的声明中发表任何声明。此后，该公司重新设计了MCU，使其使用64GB eMMC，因此不应影响最新的车辆。较流行的Model 3和Y不受此问题的影响。
  特斯拉是在保修期内更换了这些配件，还是车主必须为此付费？
  它已更换了保修期内的7,777台，而非保修期内的约4,700台。 
最初，您不得不争论要在保修期内将其更换。 特斯拉（Tesla）最近通过电子邮件向所有车主发出了“澄清”的信。 它将在保修范围内。 但是，我希望许多受影响的车辆已经过保修期。 我的2014 S没有特别的问题，但是我已经超出了扩展范围。 保修（100k英里），所以如果（当）我的设备失灵，我将不得不自掏腰包。 我真的很高兴这次召回。 考虑到显示屏对汽车的操作至关重要（可通过它访问车主手册），以及特斯拉没有遵循行业最佳实践的明确程度，我同意NHTSA的评估，要求进行修复 。
note_en:
In November, we learned that the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration  had opened an engineering analysis into a potential defect with Tesla's popular Models S and X battery electric vehicles. On Wednesday, the other shoe dropped, when NHTSA informed the American automaker that it has to recall 158,000 vehicles to fix defective touchscreens.
 The problem concerns a component in the vehicles' infotainment systems, called the Media Control Unit. Buried within the MCU is an 8GB eMMC NAND flash memory chip, which can only be written to a finite number of times. Once this number of read/write cycles is reached—something that takes between three and four years depending on how much the car is driven—the touchscreen dies. And unfortunately, that's a real problem in a car where the touchscreen is the way almost all the controls are accessed.
  Not being able to browse the Internet in your car or stream a podcast is obviously an inconvenience, especially in an high-end vehicle. But NHTSA is more concerned about the fact that if the touchscreen dies, functions like the backup camera and window defogging are lost, too, as are audible alerts for other onboard safety systems. The problem is well-known among the Tesla community, affecting any Model S built between 2012 and 2018, and Model Xs built between 2016 and 2018. After NHTSA received 537 complaints from owners it asked Tesla if it had more information on the scope of the problem. It did—handing over records of thousands of complaints and more than 12,000 MCU replacements.
    Now, NHTSA has taken the unusual steps of telling Tesla it must recall the 158,000 affected vehicles,  according to Reuters. "[D]uring our review of the data, Tesla provided confirmation that all units will inevitably fail given the memory device’s finite storage capacity," NHTSA told Reuters. NHTSA also noted that Tesla had attempted to resolve the issue with software patches, but these have been "procedurally and substantially insufficient" and the law requires automakers to recall vehicles that contain safety defects.
 Tesla has no press office to contact for a comment, and the company has not made a statement on either its Twitter feed or that of CEO Elon Musk's. The company has since redesigned the MCU to use a 64GB eMMC, so more recent vehicles should not be affected. The more popular Models 3 and Y are unaffected by this issue.
  Was Tesla replacing these under warranty previously or did owners have to pay for it?
  It has replaced 7,777 under warranty and another ~4,700 not under warranty.
  Originally you had to argue to get it replaced under warranty. Tesla recently emailed all owners that they were "clarifying" that it would be covered under warranty.  However, I expect that a lot of the vehicles affected are already out of warranty. I've not had this particular issue with my 2014 S, but I'm already out of the "extended" warranty (100k mi), so I would be having to pay out of pocket if (when) my unit goes out.  I'm actually happy for this recall. Given how critical the display is to operating the car (hell, the owners manual is accessed through it) and how clearly Tesla didn't follow industry best practices, I agree with the NHTSA's assessment that a fix is required.
title: 特斯拉的触摸屏故障：NHTSA要求召回S和X型
title_en: Tesla’s touchscreen trouble: NHTSA asks for recall of Models S and X

三星的新智能手机终于正式上市，让我们认识一下Galaxy S21系列。设计和规格与传闻一致，但今天的大新闻是价格范围，希望这是一个迹象，表明2020年的天价将有所下降。
 与去年相比，Galaxy S21系列产品全面降价200美元。以前，（为清楚起见，加上一分钱）三星为S20，S20 +和S20 Ultra分别收取了1,000美元，1,200美元和1,400美元的费用。今年，S21为800美元，S21 +为1,000美元，Ultra为1,200美元。较便宜的型号可以看到一些明显的成本削减，但是我不能说足以证明200美元的价格下降是合理的-价格确实更低。而且对于Ultra模型也没有争论，这似乎仍然像＆＃34; Ultra＆＃34;和去年一样，价格更低。
 三星现在称基本型号S21为“以价值为导向”。设备，例如Note20，正在将S21改为塑料后盖，以降低材料清单成本。另外两个手机是玻璃的，据说所有产品都具有哑光效果，这听起来很适合减少指纹。
 说到规格，Galaxy S21和S21 +几乎是同一部手机。通常会在三星的新闻稿中将它们作为一对提及，而Ultra则具有专有的规格和功能。
   这三款手机均具有120Hz显示屏，今年的新功能是动态刷新率，该功能可使显示屏根据屏幕上的内容调整其刷新率。如果做得好，这将在需要时为手机提供如丝般流畅的滚动和动画效果（并在不需要时节省电池）。三星没有提供有关其工作原理的大量细节，但理想情况下，您希望滚动和其他系统生成的动画的刷新率为120Hz，60fps游戏为60Hz，24fps电影为24Hz，并且读取静态文本时大约为1Hz。
    三星对此的第一代实现不是极端的：在S20和S20 +上，显示屏可以从120Hz降至48Hz。在Ultra型号上，您可以一直降低到10Hz。去年，您只能将手机锁定在120Hz或60Hz。
 削减成本的一个迹象：这两种便宜的机型的分辨率从1440p降至1080p。 Android手机一直都有大量像素可供使用，即使是性能较差的Plus型号也仍然以393ppi的速度运行。这两款手机的RAM也越来越少：8GB，而不是去年的标准12GB。 S21和S21 Ultra的电池相同，但plus型号从4500mAh上升到4800mAh。 
在美国和其他特定地区，所有型号的手机均配备了新的高通Snapdragon 888 SoC。 Galaxy S21不是世界上第一台888设备（即小米Mi 11），但在许多地区，它将是第一台商用设备。这些电话在世界其他地方（最有可能在欧洲）使用三星的新型Exynos 2100 SoC。两者都是具有Arm新的X1内核，三个A78内核和四个A55内核的5nm SoC。主要区别将在于调制解调器和GPU，一旦电话实际发布，互联网无疑将使它们成为在线基准测试的主题。与去年相比，高通承诺将CPU的性能提高25％，将GPU的性能提高35％，将ISP的性能提高35％。
 您可能还可以以较低的价格提出Snapdragon 888的价格。去年全年，我们一直在大力宣传Snapdragon 865的设计，该设计将4G调制解调器从SoC中拉出，并将其粘贴在单独的调制解调器芯片上，所有这些也是为了获得对第一代5G的支持。公司没有谈论部件价格，但是高通芯片永远都带有板载调制解调器，而额外的865调制解调器芯片很可能会提高高通芯片封装的成本。 5G硬件占用了额外的空间和更多的功率，并且可能是为了避免困扰第一代4G手机的问题，制造商用更大的手机和更大的电池进行了补偿，从而进一步提高了价格。今年，Snapdragon 888是高通公司第一款带有板载5G调制解调器的旗舰SoC，尽管尺寸没有下降，但价格却在下降。
    在今年的美国，这三种型号都具有mmWave兼容性。这是基本型号的升级，去年仅支持6GHz以下。 mmWave很难出售，因为即使mmWave的最大推动者Verizon也仅覆盖其网络的4％。
   在我们讨论高通问题的同时，Galaxy S21配备了一个新的显示屏下超声波指纹读取器，尺寸几乎是原来的两倍。与以前的版本相比，它可以更快地扫描指纹。询问时，三星不会确认指纹读取器的型号或制造商。高通公司刚刚宣布了其下一代超声波指纹读取器：第2代3D声波传感器，其读取器面积扩大了77％，速度提高了50％。高通公司是唯一的超声波（相对于光学）指纹读取器制造商。由于三星去年使用了Qualcomm传感器，并且改进规格匹配，因此我们称其为匹配。此前，三星在S10和S20中交付了超小型的第一代指纹传感器，其面积仅为9×4mm。高通公司的新传感器更大，尺寸为8×8mm，这有助于提高精度。但是它仍然小于指尖，约14×14mm。
   今年最大的设计变更是后置摄像头，它现在已经成为手机角落的重要组成部分。三星将其称为“轮廓切割相机”外壳。与早期的投机渲染不同，玻璃不会包裹在手机的角落。那里仍然有金属，所以它不应该太易碎。与去年一样，我们有12MP主相机，12MP广角镜头和64MP长焦。长焦似乎与去年的设置相同，几乎没有1.06倍光学变焦，并且来自64MP传感器的大量数字变焦被三星称为“ Hybrid Optic 3X”。放大。
 我们在Snapdragon 888版本中发现的一个奇怪的功能是能够同时从三个不同的后置摄像头捕获数据的能力，我们想知道手机制造商会怎么做。三星已将其变成S20相机的便捷功能：相机应用程序中每个相机镜头的实时预览。该功能称为“实时缩略图”。在照片模式和导演视图下在视频模式下-用户可以看到主镜头，广角镜头和远摄镜头如何构图。希望它将吸引不那么精明的用户在他们的设备背面尝试一堆相机。
note_en:
Samsung's new smartphones are finally official, so let's meet the Galaxy S21 family. The design and specs line up with what was rumored, but the big news today is the price range, which is hopefully a sign that the sky-high prices in 2020 are coming down a bit.
 The Galaxy S21 line is seeing an across-the-board $200 price cut compared to last year. Previously, (adding a penny for clarity's sake) Samsung was charging $1,000, $1,200, and $1,400 for the S20, S20+, and S20 Ultra, respectively. This year,  the S21 is $800, the S21+ is $1,000, and the Ultra is $1,200. The cheaper models are seeing  some visible cost-cutting, but I wouldn't say it's enough to justify a $200 price drop—the price is genuinely lower. And there's no arguing this for the Ultra model, which still seems every bit as "Ultra" as last year, with a lower price.
 Samsung is now calling the base model S21 a "value-oriented" device and, like the Note20, is changing the S21 to a plastic back for a lower bill of materials. The other two phones are glass, and everything supposedly has a "matte finish," which sounds great for fingerprint reduction.
 When it comes to specs, the Galaxy S21 and S21+ are pretty much the same phone on the inside. They're usually mentioned as a pair in Samsung's press releases, while the Ultra has exclusive specs and features.
   All three phones have a 120Hz display, and new this year is a dynamic refresh rate, which lets the display adjust its refresh rate based on what is on the screen. If done well, this would give the phone silky-smooth scrolling and animations when it needs them (and save battery when it doesn't). Samsung doesn't give a ton of details about what it works with, but ideally, you'd want a 120Hz refresh rate for scrolling and other system-generated animations, 60Hz for 60fps games, 24Hz for 24fps movies, and something like 1Hz while reading static text.
    Samsung's first-gen implementation of this isn't extreme: on S20 and S20+, the display can adjust from 120Hz down to 48Hz. On the Ultra model, you can slow all the way to 10Hz. Last year, you could only lock the phone to 120Hz or 60Hz.
 One sign of cost-cutting: the two cheaper models are seeing a resolution drop from 1440p to 1080p. Android phones have always had tons of pixels to spare, and even the worse-off Plus model is still clocking in at a respectable 393ppi. The two phones are also getting less RAM: 8GB instead of the standard 12GB of last year. The battery is the same on the S21 and S21 Ultra, but the plus model is getting a bump from 4500mAh to 4800mAh.
  In the United States and other select territories, all models of the phone feature the new  Qualcomm Snapdragon 888 SoC. The Galaxy S21 isn't the first 888 device in the world (that's the  Xiaomi Mi 11), but for many territories, it will be the first commercial device. Elsewhere in the world, most likely in Europe, the phones use Samsung's new  Exynos 2100 SoC. Both are 5nm SoCs with Arm's new X1 core, three A78 cores, and four A55 cores. The major differences will be in the modems and GPUs, which the Internet will undoubtedly make the subject of online benchmark battles once the phones actually release. Compared to last year, Qualcomm is promising performance improvements of 25 percent from the CPU, 35 percent from the GPU, and 35 percent from the ISP.
 You can probably also file the Snapdragon 888 under "reasons for a lower price." All last year, we were harping on the  Snapdragon 865's design, which pulled the 4G modem off of the SoC and stuck it on a separate modem chip, all for the sake of also bolting on first-gen 5G support. Companies don't talk about parts prices, but Qualcomm chips have had onboard modems forever, and the 865's extra modem chip most likely raised the cost of Qualcomm's chip package. The 5G hardware took up extra space and more power, and probably to avoid the problems  that plagued first-gen 4G phones, manufacturers compensated with larger phones and bigger batteries, further raising the price. This year, the Snapdragon 888 is Qualcomm's first flagship SoC with an onboard 5G modem, and while the sizes aren't going down, the prices are.
    In the United States this year, all three models are getting mmWave compatibility. This is an upgrade for the base model, which last year only supported sub-6GHz. mmWave is a tough sell since even the biggest pusher of mmWave, Verizon, only has coverage in  4 percent of its network.
   While we're on the subject of Qualcomm, the Galaxy S21 has a new under-display ultrasonic fingerprint reader that is "nearly double in size" compared to the previous version, and it can scan your fingerprint faster. Samsung would not confirm the model or manufacturer of the fingerprint reader when asked. Qualcomm just announced its next-generation ultrasonic fingerprint reader, though: the  3D Sonic Sensor Gen 2, which features a 77 percent larger reader area and is 50 percent faster. Qualcomm is just about the only manufacturer of ultrasonic (as opposed to optical) fingerprint readers. Since Samsung used a Qualcomm sensor last year and the improvement specs match, we're calling it a match. Previously, Samsung shipped a super-tiny first-generation fingerprint sensor in the S10 and S20, with an area of only 9×4mm. Qualcomm's new sensor is a bigger 8×8mm, which should help with accuracy. But it's still smaller than a fingertip, which is around 14×14mm.
   The big design change this year is the rear camera, which is now a big block that is part of the corner of the phone. Samsung calls it the "Contour Cut Camera housing." Unlike the early speculative renders, the glass doesn't wrap around the corner of the phone. There's still metal there, so it shouldn't be too smashable. Like last year, we've got a 12MP main camera, a 12MP wide-angle, and a 64MP Telephoto. The telephoto seems to be the same setup as last year, with a barely-there 1.06x optical zoom and lots of digital zoom from the 64MP sensor, in a package Samsung is calling a "Hybrid Optic 3X" zoom.
 One of the stranger features we spotted in the Snapdragon 888 release was the ability to simultaneously capture data from three different rear cameras, and we wondered what phone manufacturers would do with that. Samsung has turned it into a handy feature in the S20 camera: live previews of each camera lens in the camera app. The feature—called "Live Thumbnails" in photo mode and "Director's View" in video mode—will let users see how the main, wide-angle, and telephoto lens would all frame a shot. Hopefully, it will invite less-savvy users to try out the pile of cameras on the back of their device.
title: Galaxy S21正式发布，价格全面下调200美元
title_en: The Galaxy S21 is official, gets an across-the-board $200 price drop

首席执行官约翰·马特泽（John Matze）在周三的采访中告诉路透社，帕勒（Parler）可能永远不会从亚马逊和其他许多科技公司的禁令中恢复过来。
 “我是个乐观主义者，＆＃34;他在谈话的某一时刻说。 ＆＃34;可能要花几天，可能要花几周，但Parler会回来，而当我们这样做时，我们会变得更强壮。”
 但是在谈话的另一点，他承认，这可能永远不会。我们还不知道。＆＃34;
 周末，谷歌和苹果都将Parler从他们的应用商店中删除了。此后不久，Amazon将Parler从托管该站点的Amazon Web Services上撤下。不只是亚马逊。 ＆＃34;从短信服务到电子邮件提供商再到我们的律师，每个供应商都抛弃了我们，＆＃34; Matze在周日接受Fox News采访时说。从那时起，Parler一直处于离线状态。
  科技公司之所以担心，是因为帕勒已经成为计划在上周对美国国会大厦进行暴力袭击的右翼极端分子的聚集地。亚马逊表示，它就网站上托管的暴力内容警告了Parler数月之久，例如，该内容要求对指定官员和私人公民进行强奸，酷刑和暗杀。
 亚马逊从AWS启动Parler后，Parler起诉了亚马逊。帕勒（Parler）声称，此次撤职是反竞争阴谋的一部分，旨在增强Twitter的统治地位。但是正如亚马逊在回应中指出的那样，亚马逊没有托管Twitter的feed，而两家公司也没有就Parler进行交流。
 随着多个服务同时放弃Parler，Parler的工程师可能需要一段时间才能使用不同的供应商来重建服务。即使他们能够做到这一点，也很可能会受到苹果和Google禁令的阻碍，因为他们将无法轻松地向用户分发其移动应用程序。
note_en:
Parler may never recover from being banned by Amazon and a number of other technology companies, CEO John Matze told Reuters in a  Wednesday interview.
 “I am an optimist," he said at one point in the conversation. "It may take days, it may take weeks but Parler will return and when we do we will be stronger.”
 But at another point in the conversation, he acknowledged, "It could be never. We don’t know yet."
 Over the weekend,  Google and Apple both removed Parler from their app stores. Shortly after that, Amazon  kicked Parler off of Amazon Web Services, which was hosting the site. And it wasn't just Amazon. "Every vendor, from text message services to email providers to our lawyers, all ditched us," Matze  said in a Sunday interview on Fox News. Parler has been offline ever since then.
  Technology companies were concerned because Parler had become a gathering place for the kind of right-wing extremists who planned last week's violent assault on the US Capitol. Amazon says it  warned Parler for months about violent content hosted on the site—for example, content that called for "the rape, torture, and assassination of named public officials and private citizens."
 After Amazon booted Parler off AWS,  Parler sued Amazon. Parler claims that the takedown was part of an anticompetitive conspiracy to bolster Twitter's dominance. But as Amazon pointed out in its  response, Amazon doesn't host Twitter's feed, and the two companies didn't communicate about Parler.
 With multiple services ditching Parler simultaneously, it could take a while for Parler's engineers to rebuild the service using different vendors. Even if they can manage that, they're likely to be stymied by the Apple and Google bans, since they'll have no easy way to distribute their mobile app to users.
title: Parler CEO承认该网站可能永远无法从亚马逊禁令中恢复
title_en: Parler CEO admits site may never recover from Amazon ban

根据美国国家公路交通安全管理局，美国的道路在2020年变得更加致命。尽管无法获得全年的数字，但美国国家公路交通安全管理局（NHTSA）已计算出2020年前9个月我们道路上的死亡人数，这一消息十分严峻。在去年1月初至9月底之间，有28,190人在撞车事故中丧生，在2019年的同一9个月中增长了4.6％，或增加了1,249例死亡。（2020年的完整统计数据赢得了＃39 ；直到今年晚些时候才可用。）
 使这种增加的消息变得更糟的是，事实上，2019年的道路交通事故死亡人数减少了，与2018年相比下降了2％。实际上，当NHTSA在去年12月下旬发布2019年的统计数据时，对2020年上半年的分析发现，在这六个月中，同期的交通量下降了16％，道路死亡人数下降了3.3％。
     （由于行进的行驶里程的减少大于死亡的减少，因此与2019年1月和2020年上半年相比，每行驶1亿英里的死亡率实际上从1.06增加到1.25。） 7月和9月，死亡人数大幅上升了13.3％。罪魁祸首？腾空道路，鼓励驾驶员提速。整个2020年，多个州都报告了超速驾驶罚单的大幅增加，特别是发给时速超过25mph（40km / h）或更高的驾驶员的罚单。更糟糕的是，似乎有更多的驾驶员在撞车时发现系统中有毒品或酒精，而戴安全带的人更少。
  在全国范围内，医院在CO​​VID-19大流行的压力下屈服，其重症监护病房完全满员，没有多余的能力来应对开车太快然后撞车的人。仅仅因为道路是空的，并不能使它们成为您的私人比赛跑道-放慢脚步并保持明智，因为如果您确实撞上车祸，您可能会发现医院实际上无法挽救您的生命。
  这一直是我们州进行对话和地方政府警告的话题，尽管在这一部分进行了一些微调：7月至9月的几个月里情况变得越来越糟，死亡人数大幅增加了13.3％。罪魁祸首？腾空道路，鼓励驾驶员提速。
 至少我们的国家指导方针是，它不仅只是空荡荡的道路，事实上交通已经恢复了，但是空荡荡的道路习惯和明显减少的执法行动仍然存在。因此，很多人已经习惯了在完全锁定期间更加危险地驾驶，但是后来这些人解除了。由于可能要进行永久性的在家上班，并且许多零售商和聚集点仍在此处关闭，因此一些交通量已经大大减少，但是没有什么比真正的低点高。显然，对于某些人来说，不良习惯是棘手的一种方法，这真是令人不寒而栗。
note_en:
American roads became significantly deadlier in 2020, according to the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration. Although figures for the entire year won't be available for some time, the NHTSA has calculated the death toll on our roads for the first nine months of 2020, and the news is grim. Between the beginning of January and the end of September last year, 28,190 people were killed in crashes, which is an increase of 4.6 percent—or 1,249 more deaths—over the same nine months in 2019. (The full statistics for 2020 won't be available until later this year.)
 What makes this increase even worse news is the fact that 2019 actually saw a  decrease in road deaths,  which declined by two percent compared to 2018. In fact, when the NHTSA released the 2019 statistics in late December of last year, it included a preliminary analysis of the first half of 2020 and found that during those six months, the traffic volume decreased by 16 percent, and road deaths declined by 3.3 percent over the same time period.
     (As the decrease in vehicle miles traveled was greater than the decrease in deaths, the fatality rate per 100 million vehicle miles traveled actually increased from 1.06 to 1.25 when comparing 1H 2019 and 1H 2020.) Things got much, much worse during the months between July and September, with deaths rising by a massive 13.3 percent. The primary culprit? Emptier roads, encouraging drivers to speed. Throughout 2020,  multiple states have reported big increases in speeding tickets, and particularly those issued to drivers going 25mph (40km/h) or more above the speed limit. To make matters worse, it seems that more of the drivers getting into crashes are found with drugs or alcohol in their system, and fewer have been wearing seatbelts.
  Around the country,  hospitals are buckling under the strain of the COVID-19 pandemic, with completely full intensive care units and no spare capacity to deal with people who drove way too fast and then crashed. Just because the roads are empty, that does not make them your private race tracks—slow down and be sensible, because if you do get into a crash, you might find the hospital can't actually save your life.
  This has been a reason topic of conversation and local government warnings in our state, though with a slight tweak from this part:   Things got much, much worse during the months between July and September, with deaths rising by a massive 13.3 percent. The primary culprit? Emptier roads, encouraging drivers to speed.
 What our state guidance at least was that it hasn't just been emptier roads, it's that traffic has in fact come back, yet  habits from emptier roads and significantly reduced enforcement have remained. So a lot of people got used to driving more dangerously during the full lockdowns, but those have since lifted. There has been a significant reduction in some traffic due to possibly permanent work-from-home shifts and many retailers and gathering spots remaining closed here, but nothing like the real low point. It's definitely been a bummer to read that apparently for some people bad habits are a one way ratchet  .
title: 尽管COVID-19被封锁，但美国道路却变得更加危险
title_en: Despite COVID-19 lockdowns, American roads are getting much deadlier

 与NES和SNES Classic Edition游戏机不同，通过直接USB电缆连接进行黑客攻击相对容易，Game＆amp;观看：超级马里奥兄弟装置具有锁定的CPU，AES加密的闪存，并且没有通过USB-C充电器进行数据连接。破解游戏与游戏因此，Watch当前需要破解系统并使用定制硬件来回转储定制固件和自制软件。
 Stacksmashing告诉Gizmodo，他们正在编辑有问题的视频，并将提出争议，以尝试将其恢复使用。任天堂未回复Ars Technica的置评请求。
  那是您什至可以版权保护的东西吗？我不了解版权的哪一部分？任天堂声称拥有吗？ 
任天堂对视频游戏，其视觉外观，声音，故事情节等拥有版权。因此，基于视频游戏，其视频游戏的游戏记录的任何记录都将是衍生作品，因此可能侵犯其版权。原始作品的版权。结果，任天堂以及几乎任何视频游戏开发商/发行商都可以对任何展示其游戏之一的游戏玩法的视频提出版权侵犯。实际上，大多数开发者/发布者都不会撤下游戏视频，而经常积极鼓励它，因为他们不会将视频视为实际游戏的替代品，并且经常会看到这种视频增加观看者的销量选择自己玩游戏。在这种情况下，任天堂利用这样的事实：显示内置游戏的某些游戏玩法的黑客录像在技术上侵犯了版权，因此提出了DMCA请求以撤消录像。发出侵权投诉时，可能的辩护是宣布合理使用，但这仅在使用版权作品对以后的作品至关重要时才适用。因此，例如，游戏评论可以证明公平地展示游戏画面来评论游戏的质量是合理的。由于不一定要显示内置游戏来演示如何破解手持设备，因此在这种情况下，这可能不是一个引人注目的论点。

Nintendo is using copyright strikes to take down YouTube videos that detail how to hack the recently released  Game & Watch: Super Mario Bros. color handheld.
 A hacker going by the handle stacksmashing managed to hack the portable unit  a day before its official November 13 release, thanks to an early retail delivery. But a YouTube video detailing that hacking method,  originally posted on November 14, was  taken down by a  targeted copyright claim from Nintendo earlier this week. Another stacksmashing video, entitled "Bringing homebrew to the Nintendo Game & Watch," has also been taken down by an apparent copyright claim.
 Two other stacksmashing videos on Game & Watch hacking remain up as of press time: one detailing  how to load a  Doom port onto the unit, and one  discussing how to dump the firmware. Neither of these extant videos features footage of Nintendo's own games shown running on the Game & Watch; such footage is specifically blurred out in one of the videos, in fact.
 Nintendo doesn't seem to be automatically targeting all  Game & Watch:  Super Mario Bros. gameplay footage on YouTube; you can still find  plenty of videos reviewing the unit while featuring copyrighted footage, for instance. But Nintendo's copyright on the games themselves  gives the company incredibly broad discretion on which "performances" of those games to allow or block through the Digital Millennium Copyright Act.
 Unlike the NES and SNES Classic Edition consoles, which were  relatively  simple to hack via a direct USB cable connection, the  Game & Watch: Super Mario Bros. unit features a locked CPU, AES-encrypted flash memory, and no data connection through the USB-C charger. Hacking the Game & Watch thus currently requires cracking open the system and  using custom hardware to dump custom firmware and homebrew software back and forth.
 Stacksmashing  tells Gizmodo that they're editing the videos in question and will file disputes in an attempt to get them back on the service. Nintendo has not responded to a request for comment from Ars Technica.
  Is that something you can even copyright? I don't understand what part of the "copyright" does Nintendo claim to have?
 Nintendo has a copyright on the video games, their visual appearance, sound, storyline, etc.   As such, any recording of the gameplay of their video games are going to be a derivative work, based on the video game, and thus potentially infringing on the copyright of the originating work. As a result, Nintendo, and really any video game developer/publisher, can claim copyright infringement on any video showing gameplay from one of their games.  In reality, most developers/publishers don't take down gameplay videos, and often actively encourage it, because they don't view the videos as a replacement for the actual game, and often see such videos increase sales as viewers opt to play the game for themselves.  In this instance, Nintendo is using the fact that the hacking videos that show some gameplay of the built-in games are technically copyright infringement to file DMCA requests to get the videos take down.   When infringement complaints are issued, a possible defense is to declare fair use, but that largely only applies for when using the copyrighted work is essential to the later work. So, for example, a game review can argue it's fair to show footage of the game to comment about the game's quality. Since it's not essential to show the built-in games in order to show how to hack the handheld, that might not be a compelling argument in this instance.
title: 任天堂使用版权声明删除游戏和观看黑客视频
title_en: Nintendo uses copyright claims to take down Game & Watch hacking videos

在本周的消费电子展期间，研究公司IDC发布了一份有关2020年全球传统PC销量的报告，它讲述的故事比我们过去几年习惯的要多。 2020年第四季度，PC出货量比去年同期增长26.1％。
 这意味着整体同比增长13.1％，这是相当长一段时间以来PC销售的最佳年份和月份。到2020年第四季度，总共出货了9160万台传统PC。 IDC报告中的内容包括台式机，笔记本电脑和工作站等系统。多年来，即使其他更新的计算小工具类别（如智能手机，智能扬声器和平板电脑）的增长相对较快，这类计算机的销售却在最坏的情况下下降或在最好的情况下增长可以忽略不计。
  IDC指出，市场上次看到这种增长是在2010年，当时现代多点触摸智能手机仍在发展，而Apple的第一台iPad才刚刚推出。毫无疑问，增长主要是围绕家庭和远程学习需求的工作而来的，根据报告。但它也指出，与此无关的细分市场，例如游戏PC和显示器，在这一年中也实现了显着增长。总体增长也部分是由于基于Chrome的设备正在从教育领域扩展到消费市场这一事实。根据IDC副总裁Ryan Reith的说法。
    该公司预测，这一增长尚未完成，并表示销售有望在一段时间内继续增长。
 还有市场份额的故事。就2020年全球PC出货量而言，排名前五位的公司分别是联想（24％市场份额），惠普（22.4％），戴尔（16.6％），苹果（7.6％）和宏cer（6.9％） ）。到2020年，这些市场份额数字与前一年没有显着差异。苹果和宏cer的年度增幅最大，而惠普和戴尔的增幅较小。市场领导者联想在同比增长方面处于中等水平。
 对于那些在2020年追随该行业的人们而言，传统PC在2020年的整体大幅增长只是在经济受到整体打击和失业的情况下感到惊讶。毫不奇怪，人们花了更多的钱在重型机器上进行创意工作或高度沉浸式娱乐，而其他类型的设备在用户在家里呆在家里时却没用大流行。
note_en:
During the Consumer Electronics Show this week, research firm IDC released a report on worldwide traditional PC sales in 2020, and it tells a rosier story than we've been used to in recent years. In the fourth quarter of 2020, PC shipments grew 26.1 percent over the same period last year.
 That means 13.1 percent year-over-year growth overall, and the best year and month for PC sales in quite some time. In total, 91.6 million traditional PCs were shipped in the fourth quarter of 2020. "Traditional PCs" in IDC's report include systems like desktops, laptops, and work stations. For years, sales of these kinds of computers  were declining at worst or growing negligibly at best even as other, newer computing gadget categories like smartphones, smart speakers, and tablets grew relatively rapidly.
  IDC  notes that the last time the market saw this kind of growth was way back in 2010, when modern multitouch smartphones were still building momentum and Apple's very first iPad had only just launched. The growth was unsurprisingly largely "centered around work from home and remote learning needs," according to the report. But it also notes that segments unrelated to that, like gaming PCs and monitors, also saw significant growth over the course of the year. The overall growth is also partly due to the fact that "Chrome-based devices are expanding beyond education into the consumer market," according to IDC Vice President Ryan Reith.
    The firm predicts that the surge is not yet complete and says that sales are expected to continue to grow for a while still.
 There's also the market share story. The top five companies in terms of worldwide PC shipments in 2020, from top to bottom, were Lenovo (24 percent market share), HP (22.4 percent), Dell (16.6 percent), Apple (7.6 percent), and Acer (6.9 percent). These market share numbers were not dramatically different in 2020 than they were in the prior year. Apple and Acer saw the most substantial growth year over year, while HP and Dell posted somewhat more modest growth numbers. Market leader Lenovo fell in the middle of the pack in terms of year-over-year growth.
 For those following the industry in 2020, the overall big growth for traditional PCs in 2020 only comes as a surprise in the context of overall hits to the economy and job losses. It's no surprise at all that people have been spending more on heavy-duty machines for doing creative work or highly immersive entertainment as opposed to other kinds of devices that aren't as useful when users are stuck at home during a pandemic.
title: 经过多年的稳步下降，个人电脑销售终于在2020年出现了大幅增长
title_en: PC sales finally saw big growth in 2020 after years of steady decline

Update, 1/14: A trio of new reports make clear that contrary to Facebook COO Sheryl Sandberg's position, individuals were using Facebook to plan violence before last week's insurrection at the US Capitol—and that users are still doing so today.
 The New York Times today  published a report looking at individuals, including at least one who attended the January 6 rally at the Capitol, who were radicalized specifically on Facebook and Instagram. Simply put, many users whose earlier content tended toward the bland and anodyne saw massive spikes in engagement—way more likes and comments—from other users when they began sharing conspiracy theories alleging the 2020 election was "stolen" or other Qanon-style content.
 Many users "transformed seemingly overnight," according to the NYT review. "A decade ago, their online personas looked nothing like their presences today. A journey through their feeds offers a glimpse of how Facebook rewards exaggerations and lies."
 Data scientists and executives inside the company were aware that violence was proliferating on Facebook and Instagram before and during the events of January 6, according to  the Wall Street Journal. Reports of violent content on the platforms that afternoon "jumped more than 10-fold from the morning," according to internal documents WSJ reporters reviewed.
 During the chaotic afternoon, the WSJ reports, Facebook Chief Technology Officer Mike Schroepfer urged employees to "hang in there," while the company worked out how "to allow for peaceful discussion and organizing but not calls for violence."
 Some employees, however, were unmoved by this plea for patience. "All due respect, but haven’t we had enough time to figure out how to manage discourse without enabling violence?" one reportedly responded. "We’ve been fueling this fire for a long time and we shouldn’t be surprised that it’s now out of control."
 Violent rhetoric on Facebook remains a major problem today,  Reuters reports, with threats against the upcoming inauguration of President-elect Joe Biden proliferating.
 A Facebook spokesperson, who asked not to be named for "security reasons," told Reuters that the insurrection at the Capitol appeared to be a "galvanizing event," and efforts on Facebook to bring Trump supporters back to Washington, DC, next week are now widespread. "Signals Facebook tracked included digital flyers promoting the events, some of them featuring calls to arms or the insignia of militias or hate groups," Reuters reports.
    Several Facebook events, pages, and groups designed to bring crowds of Trump supporters to the Capitol around Biden's inauguration that we reported on below on Tuesday are still active on the site as of Thursday.
  Facebook executive leaders promise they are doing everything they can to prevent the platform from being a tool for an  anticipated new wave of violence in the nation's capital in the upcoming days. At the same time, however, content threatening violence is still up on Facebook, and those same executives are downplaying how big a role the platform had in  last week's events at the US Capitol.
 Facebook COO Sheryl Sandberg gave  a livestreamed interview with Reuters Monday in which she said President Donald Trump is not likely to get his Facebook account reinstated following the company's " indefinite" ban.
  "Even the president is not above the polices we have," Sandberg said. "We have no plans to let him [back] in."
 But even though she reiterated that the president was banned specifically for fomenting violence on the platform, Sandberg downplayed the role Facebook may have had in bringing the mob to the Capitol in the first place.
 "We know this was organized online," Sandberg told Reuters. "We took down QAnon, Proud Boys, Stop the Steal—anything that was talking about possible violence last week. Our enforcement's never perfect, so I'm sure there were still things on Facebook, [but] I think these events were largely organized on platforms that don't have our abilities to stop hate and don't have our standards and don't have our transparency."
 Still, she added, "Certainly, to this day, we are working to find any single mention that might be linked to this and making sure we get it down as quickly as possible."
 It is true that a great deal of planning the various groups and individuals did last week took place on less-mainstream communication platforms, such as  Parler, Telegram, Gab, 4chan, and dedicated pro-Trump forums. However, the insurrectionists responsible for the attack were not  exclusively on those platforms—and the mainstream political figures who egged them on were explicitly present on mainstream platforms, including Twitter and Facebook.
 Facebook clearly knows it has a problem with violent users and content; otherwise, the platform wouldn't have needed to take the actions it has taken this week.
  Chief among those supporters of the mob was Trump himself, as Facebook CEO Mark Zuckerberg acknowledged last week when Facebook deleted a few of Trump's posts and  suspended his account.
     Trump's decision to use Facebook "to condone rather than condemn the actions of his supporters at the Capitol building has rightly disturbed people in the US and around the world," Zuckerberg said at the time. "We removed these statements yesterday because we judged that their effect—and likely their intent—would be to provoke further violence."
 Facebook is also apparently quietly concerned that violence may spill over into retaliation against company employees. Facebook's security team this week told workers to "avoid wearing or carrying Facebook-branded items at this time." The Information  was first to report on the message, which was seemingly aimed at reducing the chance of Facebook employees becoming targets.
 As part of its " preparations" for Inauguration Day, Facebook on Monday said it would  ban the phrase "stop the steal," a rallying cry used by those who don't (or don't want to) believe in the fact that President-elect Joe Biden won the election (a group that includes President Trump).
  We've had emergency measures in place since before the US elections such as not recommending civic groups for people to join. Last week, we implemented several additional ones, including increasing the requirement of Group admins to review and approve posts before they can go up, automatically disabling comments on posts in Groups that start to have a high rate of hate speech or content that incites violence, and using AI to further demote content that likely violates our policies. We're keeping these measures in place.
  Facebook, however, is clearly failing to go after "any single mention" of statements that could provoke or contribute to further violence, despite its increased efforts.
    A cursory search of Facebook "events" in and near Washington, DC, next week brought up several gatherings and demonstrations that seem designed to interfere with Biden's inauguration. Some are planned over the weekend, in the days ahead of the event, while others call for participants to gather at the Capitol on January 20—Inauguration Day itself.
 The same quick, cursory, five-minute search also brought up a half-dozen private groups that say they plan to bring together "patriots" to march on Washington during the week of the Inauguration, as well as one public group where members were posting threatening content such as a picture of House Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.), with the text, "WHY can't 'someone' shut this POS's mouth??? ... SHE ABSOLUTELY NEEDS TO BE LEAD [sic] OUT TO PASTURE."
 Neither is Facebook's block on "stop the steal" working terribly well as of yet, as Ars' own Timothy Lee  has discovered.
 In the meantime, the District of Columbia is  already shutting down for blocks around the Capitol, and heavy law enforcement and street closures are expected to remain in place through January 24.
  Deplatforming these guys wont make them go away, and I wonder how they * corporate media and social media* are going to use these policies to say things like "supporting BDS of Israel is antisemetic".   Can anyone say with confidence they wont use this to advance the policy ideas of their biggest donors?
 Sigh.  For the umpteen-thousandth time:  deplatforming these people is not supposed to make them vanish. It is supposed to prevent them from being able to recruit people in plain sight within the public sphere. The fewer platforms that allow them and the fewer places that they get to push their conspiracies, their bile, and their hate, the less opportunities that they have to suck more gullible people into their violent, dangerous bullshit.  And, for the umpteen-thousandth time:  these platforms have already shown a willingness to suppress or even silence speech for their own benefit. That's  one of the main parts of how we got here in the first place. It is not only pointless but  absurd to worry about where a crackdown on speech that led to an  attempted coup might lead, because they've been pushing against various forms of legitimate legal speech for  ages now.
title: 报道发现，Facebook在暴动前就了解暴力极端主义分子[更新]
title_en: Facebook knew about violent extremists before insurrection, reports find [Updated]

note:
中国当局阻止了世界卫生组织派遣到中国武汉的两名科学家，对新型冠状病毒的起源进行调查。然而，中国政府对卫生机构努力理解这种毁灭性大流行是如何引发的又一障碍。
 两位科学家作为一个由15人组成的国际团队的一部分，该团队长期致力于进入2019年底首次出现该病毒的城市。十三位科学家-他们多次对冠状病毒SARS-CoV呈阴性反应-2，在他们的祖国-被允许继续旅行。世卫组织今天早些时候在推特上说，他们现在已经抵达武汉。但是，中国仍然要求这13名科学家在武汉进行14天的检疫。
 两位科学家在旅行前也多次对该病毒进行了阴性测试。但是在新加坡前往武汉的途中，他们的SARS-CoV-2抗体呈阳性反应，表明该病毒过去曾暴露于该病毒。尽管PCR测试结果阴性，但中国当局还是将其留在新加坡进行其他测试。
 单独来看，两名科学家的the持似乎是一个很小的旅行障碍。但这只是科学研究中研究SARS-CoV-2如何从动物跃入人类的最新障碍。
 去年，中国驳回了澳大利亚，美国和其他国家的要求，让独立的科研人员进入武汉研究该病毒的来源。 7月，中国政府允许两名世卫组织调查人员进入中国三个星期，以“为调查该病毒的来源奠定基础”。但是，两名调查人员在逗留期间任何时候均不得访问武汉。
     本月初，更大的世卫组织团队原计划出发去武汉旅行，只是在最后一刻取消了他们的计划。世卫组织总干事特德罗斯·阿德南·格布雷耶苏斯（Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus）在1月5日的新闻发布会上说，该团队之行的依据是“世卫组织，中国政府与该团队打算前往武汉的国家之间共同制定的安排”。但是“今天，我们了解到中国官员尚未最终确定车队抵达中国的必要许可。鉴于两名成员已经开始旅行，而其他成员无法在最后一刻旅行，我对这个消息感到非常失望。”
 中国显然对提早批评和审查其大流行的早期处理持谨慎态度。但是，其为阻止调查而进行的持续努力可能只会助长有关SARS-CoV-2从中国实验室逃脱的持续性建议。尽管很难完全排除这种可能性，但用于支持实验室逃脱想法的证据充其量只能视情况而定，而最坏的情况则是wild测。 
冠状病毒专家长期以来反复争论说，大流行的可能性更大的原因是病毒跳入人类，就像其他引起爆发的冠状病毒一样。 此外，独立研究人员说，SARS-CoV-2的遗传图谱中没有任何人类动摇的迹象。
note_en:
Chinese authorities have held up two scientists sent to Wuhan, China, by the World Health Organization to investigate the origins of the novel coronavirus—yet another obstacle the Chinese government has thrown at the health agency’s efforts to understand how the devastating pandemic began.
 The two scientists were traveling as part of an international team of 15 that has long been working to gain access to the city where the virus first emerged in late 2019. Thirteen of the scientists—who all repeatedly tested negative for the coronavirus, SARS-CoV-2, in their home countries—were allowed to travel on. They have now arrived in Wuhan, the  WHO said on Twitter earlier today. However, China is still requiring the 13 scientists to undergo a 14-day quarantine in Wuhan.
 The two scientists held back also tested negative for the virus multiple times prior to traveling. But while in Singapore en route to Wuhan, they tested positive for SARS-CoV-2 antibodies, suggesting past exposure to the virus. Despite their negative PCR tests, Chinese authorities had them remain in Singapore for additional testing.
 On its own, the holdup of two scientists may seem like a minor travel snag. But it’s just the latest obstacle in the scientific effort to study how SARS-CoV-2 jumped from animals to humans.
 Last year, China dismissed requests from Australia, the US, and other countries to let independent scientific investigators have access to Wuhan to study the origins of the virus. In July, the Chinese government allowed  two WHO investigators into the country for three weeks to “lay the groundwork for an investigation into the source of the virus.” However, the two investigators were not permitted to visit Wuhan at any point in their stay.
     Earlier this month, the larger WHO team had planned to set out on a trip to Wuhan, only to have their plans cancelled at the last minute. The team’s trip was based on “arrangements jointly developed between WHO, the Chinese government, and countries for which the team was meant to travel through on their way to Wuhan,” WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said in  a January 5 press briefing. But “today we learned that Chinese officials have not yet finalized the necessary permissions for the team’s arrival in China. I am very disappointed with this news given that two members had already begun their journeys and others were not able to travel at the last minute.”
 China is apparently wary of refreshing criticism and scrutiny of its early handling of the pandemic. However, its continual effort to hamper the investigation will likely only fuel the  persistent suggestions that SARS-CoV-2 escaped from a Chinese lab. Though it’s difficult to entirely rule out the possibility, the evidence used to support the lab-escape ideas are circumstantial at best and wild speculation at worst.
 Coronavirus experts have long and repeatedly argued that the far more likely cause of the pandemic was the virus jumping species into humans, as was seen with other outbreak-causing coronaviruses. In addition, independent researchers say there are  no telltale signs of human tinkering in SARS-CoV-2’s genetic blueprints.
title: 中国继续挫败世卫组织对病毒起源的调查，科学家陷于困境
title_en: Scientists stuck as China keeps foiling WHO investigation on virus origin
